How we work

Lab Guide

A public, searchable knowledge base spanning onboarding, day-to-day lab life, the research workflow, scientific writing, and professional development. This guide is ever-evolving. Members are encouraged to improve it, and it's open so prospective members can see how the lab really works.

Pages with your word in the title come first, then pages that mention it in the text.

No guide pages match that yet. Try a shorter or different word.

Getting Started

Start guide: Undergraduates

A first-quarter checklist for new undergraduate researchers joining the lab.

Read →

Start guide: Graduate students

A first-year checklist for new graduate students, from onboarding to the preliminary exam.

Read →

Start guide: Postdocs

A first-weeks checklist for incoming postdoctoral scholars.

Read →

Working in the Lab

Work expectations

Work hours, flexibility, compensation, and how we manage projects and deadlines.

Read →

Time off & vacation

How to request leave, sick days, and notice guidelines. Rest is encouraged.

Read →

Support resources

Confidential support and reporting resources at UC Davis for every lab member.

Read →

Be safe

Lab safety culture, PPE, required trainings, and facility-specific requirements.

Read →

Pay for things

How to purchase lab items and get reimbursed — preferred routes and approvals.

Read →

Travel

Policy for booking and reimbursing travel for official lab purposes.

Read →

Ship packages

How to request a shipping label and send a package through AggieShip.

Read →

Lab operations roles

How we share lightweight ownership of the operational tasks that keep the lab running.

Read →

Research Workflow

Develop a research hypothesis

How to write clear, testable hypotheses for your thesis or project.

Read →

Review the literature

How to read and organize the literature without getting overwhelmed.

Read →

Develop quarterly SMART goals

A template for turning a rough goal into a specific, measurable, time-bound plan each quarter.

Read →

Manage data

Data hygiene, backups, file naming, and version control expectations.

Read →

Code

Version control, coding style, documentation, and dependency management.

Read →

Analyze data

A staged approach to analysis — clean, explore, quantify, and report with uncertainty.

Read →

Analyze error & uncertainty

The basics of error propagation, plus power-analysis and uncertainty tooling.

Read →

Use AI

The lab's rules and guidelines for using generative AI responsibly in research.

Read →

Writing & Dissemination

Write a first draft

Core principles, checklists, and authorship policy for manuscripts, preprints, and conference papers.

Read →

Design your figures

The lab's figure standards, a self-diagnosis checklist, and tools and palettes.

Read →

Paper revisions

How to respond to reviewer comments, and what to do once a paper is published.

Read →

Review papers

How to peer-review a paper critically, constructively, and ethically.

Read →

Make a poster

Tips for designing a clear, audience-appropriate conference poster.

Read →

Present

How to design and deliver an effective, audience-appropriate talk.

Read →

Brand & communication

When and how to use the lab and UC Davis logos in presentations and materials.

Read →

Communication & Culture

Communicating in the lab

How to reach Christina and each other, plus giving and receiving feedback and resolving conflict.

Read →

Discussion guidelines

How we hold productive, supportive discussions and maintain a brave, inclusive space.

Read →

Meetings

One-on-ones, lab meetings, and how we prepare for them.

Read →

Journal club

A regular space to discuss the meeting point of biology and engineering in flight.

Read →

Professional Development

Attending a conference

Eligibility, funding, choosing a conference, and the full approval-to-share-back workflow.

Read →

Applying for funding

How to approach fellowships and grants — what reviewers want and how to write a strong proposal.

Read →

Request recommendation letters

How to request a recommendation letter from Christina so she can write you a strong one.

Read →

File your dissertation

An overview of the UC Davis dissertation-filing process and key deadlines.

Read →

Members can also access the private Notion workspace for internal operations and onboarding material.

Bio-Informed Research & Design Lab

Department of Mechanical & Aerospace Engineering, UC Davis, Davis, CA
